title: Fix CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Column Width Calculation

statement.markdown: Given a CSS grid layout with a variable number of columns and dynamic row heights, implement a function that calculates the correct width of each column based on the grid template areas. The buggy function should take a string representing the grid template areas and return an object where the keys are the column names and the values are their respective widths. However, the function contains a subtle bug that causes incorrect column widths when the grid has more than two rows.
statement.inputFormat: The input string will be a CSS grid template area string (e.g., '[col1 col2] [col3 col4]').
statement.outputFormat: The output should be an object where the keys are the column names and the values are their respective widths.
statement.notes: The function should handle grid template areas with a variable number of columns and rows.

constraints.description: The number of rows in the grid
The number of columns in the grid

examples.explanation: The grid has two rows and four columns. The column widths are calculated based on the grid template areas.
The grid has two rows and three columns. The column widths are calculated based on the grid template areas.

hints.text: Use the CSS grid template areas to calculate the column widths. However, be aware of the grid's dynamic row heights and variable number of columns.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Grid Layout with Inconsistent Column Width Calculation
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to parse the grid template areas and calculate the column widths based on the grid's layout.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve parsing the grid template areas, iterating over each character to determine the column names, and calculating the column widths based on the grid's layout.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using a more efficient algorithm to parse the grid template areas and calculate the column widths.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a dynamic programming approach to parse the grid template areas and calculate the column widths.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by demonstrating that the calculated column widths match the expected values for various grid templates.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution can be performed by testing it with various grid templates to ensure that it produces the correct column widths.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly parsing the grid template areas
Failing to account for dynamic row heights and variable number of columns
